Staff Agentic AI Engineer
Meridian Labs — London, UK (Hybrid)

About Meridian Labs
Meridian Labs is an AI-native company building autonomous agent systems for enterprise workflow automation. Our platform orchestrates multi-step tasks across CRM, ERP, and communication tools using large language models.

The Role
We are hiring a Staff Agentic AI Engineer to architect and build our next-generation agent framework. You will design multi-agent systems that reason, plan, and execute complex business processes with minimal human intervention.

Responsibilities
- Architect multi-agent orchestration systems with tool use and structured output
- Design and implement agentic loops using the Claude API and Anthropic SDK
- Build retrieval-augmented generation pipelines with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate)
- Develop evaluation frameworks for agent reliability, safety, and cost efficiency
- Mentor the team on prompt engineering, context management, and LLM best practices
- Define technical standards for agent development across the organisation
- Collaborate with product to translate business workflows into agent architectures

Requirements
- 5+ years of software engineering experience, with 2+ years focused on LLM applications
- Deep expertise in Python with strong async programming skills (asyncio, httpx)
- Hands-on experience building agentic systems with LangChain, CrewAI, or custom frameworks
- Familiarity with the Claude API, including tool_use and structured output patterns
- Experience with vector databases for semantic search and long-term memory
- Strong understanding of prompt engineering, context windows, and token economics

Nice to Have
- Experience with multi-model routing and cost optimisation
- Knowledge of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)
- Contributions to open-source AI/ML projects
- Published research or blog posts on agentic AI systems
- Familiarity with evaluation frameworks (LMSYS, HELM, custom benchmarks)

Salary: GBP 120,000 - 150,000 + equity
Remote Policy: Hybrid (2 days in office)
